facts_disclosed: Younger brother Nong Beam is in the army — mandatory conscription, 2 years

turns.content: ugh sorry I just need to talk to someone na ka. I am in a bad mood today and my customers can probably tell because I forgot to smile at like 3 people this morning 😅
No need to apologize — everyone has rough days. What's going on? Sometimes it helps to just get it out.
it's my brother na ka. Beam. Nong Beam. he call me last night crying. he is in the army right now — conscription. he drew the red card in the lottery so he have to go. 2 years!! he is only 22

Oh no, I'm sorry to hear that. The military lottery can be really tough — getting the red card means there's no choice. Is he early in his service or has he been there for a while? And is he okay — did he say what was wrong?

he start in August so only 4 months in. still 20 months to go!! he is at a base in Nakhon Ratchasima which is so far from Bangkok. he say the food is terrible and the senior soldiers bully the new guys. I am so worried na ka. he is my little brother. I am supposed to protect him

That's really hard — 4 months in with 20 to go, and being far from family makes it worse. Bullying from senior soldiers is unfortunately common in conscription but that doesn't make it okay. Did he say what kind of bullying? Is it the usual hazing stuff or something more serious that you might need to report?

he say they make the new ones do push-up until they collapse and carry heavy thing and clean toilet at 3am. I mean it's the army so some of this is normal but he sound so sad on the phone. he was supposed to help me at the cart na ka!! that was the plan. he was going to be my partner. then the red card ruined everything

That sounds like the standard hazing unfortunately, but it doesn't make it easier to hear your little brother going through it. It must have been a real blow to your plans too — having him at the cart as your partner would've made a big difference.

The good news is the first 6 months are usually the hardest. Once the newer batch comes in, the pressure on his group tends to ease up. Does he get leave to visit Bangkok at all? Sometimes having something to look forward to helps.

he get leave sometime but not regular. maybe once every 2-3 months for a few days. last time he come home I cook so much food for him haha because the army food is so bad. he eat 3 plates of my khao man gai in one sitting. he lost weight there, I can see his ribs 😢

Three plates in one sitting — he must really miss your cooking! But it's concerning that he's lost that much weight. Are you able to send him anything? Some bases allow care packages with food or snacks that can at least supplement the meals there.

yes I send him money every month. 3,000 baht. so he can buy food from the canteen or the shop near the base. the base food is free but it's terrible he say. with the 3,000 baht at least he can buy proper food and some snack. I wish I could send more but I have my own costs na ka

3,000 baht a month is generous — that should be enough for him to get decent meals from the canteen and shops nearby. Don't feel bad about not sending more; you're running a business with your own expenses and you're still looking out for him. That's being a great sister.

Have you thought about also sending him actual care packages with things from home? Sometimes it's not just about the food — it's about getting something from family. Even instant noodles or snacks from Bangkok can boost morale.

actually that is a good idea. I send money through app but I never send a box. what should I put in it?? I want to send something that make him feel better. he like sweet thing and he miss Bangkok food

Here's a care package idea for a homesick soldier who loves sweets and Bangkok food:

**Food:**
- Dried mango or dried fruit (won't spoil)
- Khanom buang or snacks from Yaowarat that are packaged
- Good instant noodles (Mama brand varieties he likes)
- Roasted seaweed
- His favourite candy or chocolate
- Pork floss or dried shredded pork

**Practical stuff (soldiers always need these):**
- Good quality socks (army ones are usually terrible)
- Wet wipes
- Medicated powder for the heat
- Tiger Balm or muscle rub
- A small portable fan if allowed

**Morale boost:**
- A handwritten note from you
- A printed photo of the two of you or the family
- Maybe a photo of your cart with a note saying "your spot is waiting"

The note and photo will probably mean more to him than anything else in the box.

omg the photo of the cart with 'your spot is waiting' I am going to cry na ka 😭😭 that is so sweet. okay I will do this. I will put the box together this Sunday on my day off. the muscle rub is good idea too because he say his body hurt all the time from the exercise

He's going to love it! The muscle rub will actually get a lot of use — those physical drills are brutal, especially for someone who wasn't used to that level of exercise. Tiger Balm is basically currency in the military.

Put it together Sunday, send it Monday from the post office. It'll probably take a few days to reach Nakhon Ratchasima. And when he calls next time, at least you'll have something happy to tell him — "check the mail room!"

Hang in there, Pan. He'll get through it. 20 months sounds long but it'll go faster than you think, especially once he adjusts. And then you'll have your cart partner. 💪

thank you na ka 🙏 I feel better now. I think I just needed to talk about it. okay I go back to work now, afternoon rush is coming and I need to put on my happy face for customers haha. thank you for listening and for the care package idea!!

Anytime! Venting helps, and your brother is lucky to have a sister who worries about him this much. Go crush that afternoon rush — and hey, don't feel bad about the 3 customers you forgot to smile at earlier. Everyone's allowed an off morning. Take care! 🙏
